contract_review.dispatch_gate: A high-risk run is not dispatchable before exact-manifest cross-family PASS.

tasks.spec: You are an economical worker in the frontier policy lane. Boundary: you own only ./policy.md in the current task directory. Write a compact policy draft that preserves the corrected control loop, authority boundaries, and mode policy without changing any other file. The frontier orchestrator owns the immutable contract and executable checks; you do not. If the draft is missing a required policy statement, treat that as a failure and stop.
tasks.check: python3 -c 'from pathlib import Path; text = Path("policy.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8"); required = ["frontier orchestrator writes the immutable contract and executable checks", "economical proven workers implement in isolated parallel lanes", "executable checks decide PASS/FAIL", "exactly one retry receives raw failure output", "receipts drive per-task-type routing", "semantic review is conditional and can veto green but never override red", "Ringside is observational", "Paperclip is trigger/telemetry/work-product projection", "Beads/Vault remain authoritative"]; missing = [phrase for phrase in required if phrase not in text]; print("FAIL: missing: " + ", ".join(missing) if missing else "PASS: policy draft matches the lane contract"); raise SystemExit(1 if missing else 0)'
tasks.verified: policy.md preserves the corrected loop, authority boundaries, and mode policy, and the check prints named failures before exiting nonzero.

tasks.spec: You are an economical worker in the frontier template lane. Boundary: you own only ./template.md in the current task directory. Write a reusable template note that explains fill, freeze, hash, review, lint, run, integrate, semantic-review, and receipt. Do not edit sibling files or try to become the orchestrator.
tasks.check: python3 -c 'from pathlib import Path; text = Path("template.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8"); required = ["fill", "freeze", "hash", "review", "lint", "run", "integrate", "semantic-review", "receipt"]; missing = [word for word in required if word not in text] + ([] if "frontier orchestrator" in text else ["frontier orchestrator"]); print("FAIL: missing: " + ", ".join(missing) if missing else "PASS: template note covers the workflow"); raise SystemExit(1 if missing else 0)'
tasks.verified: template.md covers the workflow words and boundary text, and the check prints explicit failures instead of silently passing.
